policy.title: RBI finalizes climate scenario analysis requirements for scheduled commercial banks
policy.description: RBI released final guidelines mandating climate scenario analysis for all scheduled commercial banks with assets over ₹1 lakh crore by March 2027. Banks must incorporate physical risk metrics into ICAAP submissions and report portfolio-level heat stress and flood exposure.

policy.title: SEBI extends BRSR Core mandatory assurance to top 500 listed companies
policy.description: SEBI circular extends mandatory reasonable assurance on BRSR Core indicators to top 500 companies by market cap, effective FY2026-27. Scope 1 and 2 emissions, water intensity, and waste metrics now require third-party verification.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: Basel Committee publishes final Pillar 3 climate disclosure framework
policy.description: BCBS finalized climate-related financial risk disclosure requirements under Pillar 3, effective January 2028. Banks must disclose financed emissions for high-carbon sectors, physical risk concentrations by geography, and transition risk metrics for real estate portfolios.

policy.title: IRDAI issues draft guidelines on parametric crop and weather insurance products
policy.description: IRDAI released exposure draft allowing insurers to offer parametric products with automated payouts triggered by IMD-verified rainfall deficits exceeding 25% or temperature anomalies above 3°C. Comments due by April 30, 2026.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: ISSB announces adoption roadmap for emerging markets including India
policy.description: IFRS Foundation published jurisdiction-specific guidance for ISSB S1/S2 adoption in emerging markets, with India targeted for phased implementation starting FY2027-28. Guidance includes proportionality provisions for mid-cap companies.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk pathways
policy.description: Updated NGFS scenarios incorporate compound climate event modeling and refined damage functions for South Asia. New 'Hot House World' pathway projects 15-22% agricultural productivity decline in Indo-Gangetic plain by 2050.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for India physical risk assessment
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide ClimateScore Financial analytics for commercial real estate and infrastructure loan portfolios across 50 Indian cities. The engagement marks Jupiter's first major Indian banking client.

competitors.title: Sprih raises $8M Series A to expand climate risk module for Indian NBFCs
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed $8M Series A led by Omnivore Partners with participation from Better Capital. Funds will accelerate development of physical risk screening tools specifically designed for NBFC agricultural and MSME lending portfolios.

competitors.title: Moody's launches India-specific Physical Climate Risk Assessment for banks
competitors.description: Moody's ESG released a localized physical risk module covering district-level flood, cyclone, and heat stress projections for India. Product integrates with Moody's credit risk models and prices at $150K+ annually for enterprise licenses.

competitors.title: XDI signs MOU with State Bank of India for asset-level climate risk analytics
competitors.description: Australian climate risk firm XDI announced an MOU with SBI to pilot Gross Asset Damage analytics across the bank's ₹8 lakh crore infrastructure portfolio. Pilot covers 200 assets in coastal and flood-prone regions.

competitors.title: Watershed launches financial services module with financed emissions calculator
competitors.description: Watershed released an enterprise module for banks and asset managers featuring PCAF-aligned financed emissions calculation and ISSB S2-ready reporting templates. Platform now serves 15 global banks including two Indian private sector lenders.

competitors.title: CarbonMint partners with two regional rural banks for parametric crop insurance pilots
competitors.description: CarbonMint announced partnerships with Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka Grameena Banks to pilot blockchain-based parametric insurance for 50,000 smallholder farmers. Payouts triggered by ISRO satellite-verified drought indices.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Bank Portfolios: A Stress Testing Framework
research.description: RBI working paper estimates that unmitigated physical climate risk could increase NPAs by 1.8-3.2 percentage points for banks with high exposure to agriculture and coastal infrastructure by 2035. Recommends mandatory portfolio-level climate VaR reporting.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Lessons from Pilots
research.description: World Bank analysis of 47 parametric pilots across India, Bangladesh, and Sri Lanka finds basis risk averaging 18% for rainfall products but only 7% for temperature-triggered schemes. Recommends hybrid index designs combining satellite and ground station data.

research.title: sigma 2/2026: Natural Catastrophe Losses and Climate Risk Pricing
research.description: Swiss Re reports 2025 global insured nat-cat losses at $148B, with India experiencing $4.2B in flood and cyclone losses—up 35% year-on-year. Report notes protection gap in India exceeds 90% for climate perils.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Building Local Data Infrastructure
research.description: IFC study finds only 23% of listed companies in South Asia provide TCFD-aligned climate disclosures versus 67% in developed markets. Identifies SME supply chain emissions and water stress metrics as largest data gaps for financial institutions.

research.title: Climate Risk and Credit: Incorporating Physical Risk into PD Models
research.description: BIS Quarterly Review article demonstrates that incorporating flood and heat stress variables improves SME default prediction accuracy by 8-12% in climate-vulnerable regions. Proposes standardized approach for integrating hazard data into IRB models.

research.title: Index-Based Insurance for Climate Resilience: India Market Assessment
research.description: CRISIL estimates India's parametric insurance market could reach ₹12,000 crore by 2030, driven by bank-distributed products for agriculture and MSME sectors. Report identifies need for 30+ new weather stations per state to reduce basis risk.

ourRead.headline: Indian regulators are mandating climate risk infrastructure that banks don't have—StepChange's moment to become the default provider is narrowing fast.
body: This month's signals converge on one theme: the regulatory-to-implementation gap in Indian climate finance is closing rapidly, and global players are racing to fill it. RBI's final scenario analysis mandate, SEBI's BRSR assurance extension, and IRDAI's parametric framework together create a compliance wave hitting banks in 12-18 months. The question is no longer whether Indian FIs need climate risk tools, but who will supply them.

The RBI's March 28 guidelines are the most consequential policy development for StepChange this quarter. Mandating physical risk metrics in ICAAP submissions for large banks by March 2027 creates an immediate procurement trigger. Combined with Basel's Pillar 3 finalization requiring geographic physical risk concentration disclosures, banks face a two-front reporting challenge. StepChange should position its PD/LGD integration not as a climate product but as regulatory compliance infrastructure—the framing matters for budget allocation.

The competitor signals are alarming. Jupiter's HDFC partnership and XDI's SBI MOU represent the first serious incursions by global climate risk firms into Indian banking. Both are asset-heavy deals that will generate reference customers and India-specific model validation. Sprih's $8M raise targeting NBFCs is equally concerning—they're going after StepChange's natural expansion market. The window for establishing category leadership with tier-1 banks is narrowing; every month of delay increases the likelihood that Indian banks treat climate risk analytics as a solved problem sourced from abroad.

The BIS Quarterly Review finding that physical risk variables improve SME default prediction by 8-12% is the technical validation StepChange needs to move from pilot to production conversations. This isn't about sustainability narratives—it's about credit model performance. The RBI working paper's NPA impact estimates (1.8-3.2 percentage points) give risk officers a number they can take to board risk committees. StepChange's commercial materials should lead with these findings, not with regulatory compliance alone.

One thing to watch: the IRDAI parametric draft's comment deadline (April 30) will shape product design rules for years. One action to take: secure at least one tier-1 PSU bank as a reference customer before XDI's SBI pilot publishes results—that narrative must not be allowed to solidify.
